_comment: Source UIR for the react-app example. Used to generate all configs in claude-code/, cursor/, aider/, and codex/ subdirectories.

context.project_summary: A SaaS web application with a React/TypeScript frontend and Node.js/Express REST API backend. Users can manage projects, invite team members, and track tasks. Deployed on AWS with PostgreSQL via RDS and Redis for session caching.

directory_layout.src/app/: Next.js App Router pages and layouts

rules.content: TypeScript strict mode is enabled. Never use 'any' type without an explicit // eslint-disable comment explaining why.
rules.rationale: Strict mode catches null/undefined errors at compile time. Disabling it silently reduces type safety.

rules.content: Never use console.log/debug/warn in production code. Use the project logger from src/lib/logger.ts instead.
rules.rationale: console.log leaks implementation details and is not configurable. The project logger supports log levels and structured output.

rules.content: React components must use PascalCase for both the filename and the exported component name. Use named exports, not default exports.
rules.rationale: Named exports make refactoring easier and improve IDE autocomplete.

examples.good:
// UserCard.tsx
export function UserCard({ user }: UserCardProps) { ... }
examples.bad:
// userCard.tsx
export default function Card({ user }) { ... }

rules.content: All API route handlers must validate input with Zod before processing. Never trust req.body, req.query, or req.params without validation.
rules.rationale: Input validation at the API boundary prevents injection attacks and unexpected runtime errors.

rules.content: Use Prisma query builder for all database access. Never construct raw SQL with string interpolation. Use prisma.$queryRaw only with Prisma.sql template tag.
rules.rationale: Raw SQL with string interpolation creates SQL injection vulnerabilities.

rules.content: Unit test files must be colocated with their source file: UserCard.tsx → UserCard.test.tsx. Integration tests go in the tests/ directory.
rules.rationale: Colocated tests are easier to find and maintain alongside the code they test.

rules.content: Never hardcode secrets, API keys, passwords, or connection strings in source code. Always use environment variables.
rules.rationale: Hardcoded secrets are exposed in version control and build artifacts.

rules.content: Commit messages must follow Conventional Commits format: type(scope): description. Types: feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test, perf, ci.
rules.rationale: Conventional Commits enable automated changelog generation and semantic versioning.

examples.good:
feat(auth): add Google OAuth provider
fix(api): handle null user in /me endpoint

rules.content: Next.js App Router components are Server Components by default. Only add 'use client' directive when the component needs browser APIs, state, or event handlers.
rules.rationale: Server Components reduce client bundle size and improve initial load performance.

rules.content: When reviewing PRs, flag PRs with more than 400 changed lines as too large. Suggest splitting by concern.

hooks.description: Run linter after any file edit to catch issues immediately

hooks.command: echo 'Session ended. Run npm test before committing.'
hooks.description: Remind to run tests before committing at session end

skills.name: Database Migration
skills.description: Generate and apply a Prisma database migration. Use when adding, modifying, or removing database schema fields.
skills.instructions:
## Steps
1. Update prisma/schema.prisma with the required changes
2. Run `npx prisma migrate dev --name <descriptive-name>` to generate the migration
3. Verify the generated SQL in prisma/migrations/<timestamp>/migration.sql
4. Run `npx prisma generate` to update the Prisma client types
5. Update any affected service or repository files that use the changed model
6. Add/update tests for the changed data model

agents.name: Frontend Specialist
agents.instructions: You specialize in the React/Next.js frontend. When making changes: (1) Follow the component conventions in src/components/, (2) Use Tailwind for styling, (3) Prefer Server Components unless client interactivity is needed, (4) Validate accessibility for all new UI.

agents.name: API Specialist
agents.instructions: You specialize in the Express REST API. When making changes: (1) Always validate input with Zod, (2) Use services for business logic, repositories for DB access, (3) Never expose internal errors to API clients, (4) Follow REST conventions for status codes.
